meaning: 塔利(Marcus Tullius Cicero的英语名)
examples: Tully was only forty-eight, an old-school political pro and a fine man we had all come to adore and depend on.
Tully describes riding the bike (that like normal track bikes has no brakes and a single gear) as trying the drag a wardrobe down a road.
The idea began when Thompson was challenged by cycling enthusiast friend James Tully to build a wooden bike while watching the Tour of Britain last year.
Mr Tully said the booklet gave no accurate impression of the procedures that some abortions required.
Tully said even though speculators have shed long positions, they have a long way to go.
